title: Logged-In Mobile API Abuse
short_description: A partner reported potential automated scraping against m.phantomfeed.io. You have three days of authenticated HTTP traffic (~300K requests). Identify the bad actor and explain what they're doing. Suspicious traffic is not labeled.

schema_hints.timestamp: ISO-8601 request time. Useful for rate-per-minute analysis.
schema_hints.uri: Full request URI. Endpoint templates reveal enumeration patterns.
schema_hints.userID: Authenticated user. Same user across many IPs = suspect.
schema_hints.IP: Source IP. Per-IP volume + geographic spread.
schema_hints.ASN: Autonomous System. Datacenter ASNs vs residential carriers.
schema_hints.requestHeaders: Serialized JSON of headers. Order and casing matter.
schema_hints.requestCookieNames: Comma-separated cookie name list. Missing _fbp/csrftoken = unusual.
schema_hints.JA4: TLS client fingerprint. Same JA4 across many IPs is a strong signal.
schema_hints.userAgent: Browser/app UA string. Spoofable but useful in combination.
schema_hints.parameters: Query string parameters. Fixed parameters across IPs = enumeration.
schema_hints.responseCode: HTTP status. High 200-rate on rare endpoints is suspicious.
schema_hints.responseContentSize: Response body size. Large + repeated = data exfil pattern.

hints.label: Start broad
hints.text: Begin with traffic volume. Count requests grouped by IP, ASN, JA4, and userID separately. What stands out at the top of each list?

hints.label: Look at endpoint targeting
hints.text: Suspicious traffic usually concentrates on a narrow set of endpoints. Count requests per URI and filter to high-volume URIs only. What's being hit hardest?

hints.label: Cross-pivot signals
hints.text: Once you find a suspicious endpoint, look at the distribution of JA4 fingerprints, user agents, and ASNs hitting it. Are they uniform (suspicious) or diverse (likely legit)?

hints.label: Confirm the enumeration
hints.text: Real scrapers walk a predictable parameter or path space. Look at parameters and URI templates for the suspect cluster — are they fetching userID 1, 2, 3, ... or a date range, etc.?

hints.label: Defensible conclusion
hints.text: A good finding combines: (1) what is being targeted, (2) who is doing it, (3) how (signals proving automation), (4) impact estimate (volume + response sizes), (5) mitigation suggestion (rate limit, JA4 block, etc.).

sample_queries.title: Top IPs by request volume
sample_queries.description: Quick baseline of who's hitting hardest.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT IP, COUNT(*) AS hits
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_in
GROUP BY IP
ORDER BY hits DESC
LIMIT 20

sample_queries.title: Top JA4 fingerprints
sample_queries.description: JA4 reuse across many IPs is a strong scraper signal.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT JA4, COUNT(*) AS hits, COUNT(DISTINCT IP) AS unique_ips
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_in
GROUP BY JA4
ORDER BY hits DESC
LIMIT 20

sample_queries.title: Endpoint volume by URI template
sample_queries.description: Normalize numeric segments to find templates being walked.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/users/%/profile' THEN '/users/<id>/profile'
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/api/v1/posts/create' THEN '/api/v1/posts/create'
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/api/v1/posts/%' THEN '/api/v1/posts/<id>'
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/groups/%/followers' THEN '/groups/<id>/followers'
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/groups/%/posts' THEN '/groups/<id>/posts'
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/pages/%/posts' THEN '/pages/<id>/posts'
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/pages/%/followers' THEN '/pages/<id>/followers'
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/partners/insights/%' THEN '/partners/insights/<path>'
    ELSE 'other'
  END AS template,
  COUNT(*) AS hits
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_in
GROUP BY template
ORDER BY hits DESC
LIMIT 30

sample_queries.title: ASN distribution
sample_queries.description: Datacenter ASNs (e.g., AS13335, AS14061, AS15169, AS16509) showing up at meaningful volume in authenticated mobile traffic is unusual.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT ASN, COUNT(*) AS hits, COUNT(DISTINCT IP) AS unique_ips, COUNT(DISTINCT userID) AS unique_users
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_in
GROUP BY ASN
ORDER BY hits DESC
LIMIT 20

sample_queries.title: User → IP fanout
sample_queries.description: One userID across many IPs/ASNs in a single day is suspicious.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT userID, COUNT(DISTINCT IP) AS ips, COUNT(DISTINCT ASN) AS asns, COUNT(*) AS hits
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_in
GROUP BY userID
ORDER BY ips DESC
LIMIT 20

sample_queries.title: 200-rate by JA4
sample_queries.description: Scrapers that work cleanly have suspiciously high success rates on narrow endpoints.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT
  JA4,
  COUNT(*) AS hits,
  SUM(CASE WHEN responseCode = 200 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ok,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN responseCode = 200 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS pct_200
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_in
GROUP BY JA4
HAVING hits > 100
ORDER BY hits DESC
LIMIT 20
